candidate_problems.text: What is the precise threshold or set of necessary-and-sufficient structural conditions under which a physical system transitions from mere distributed causal coordination to genuine intentionality? The conjecture appeals to 'shared internal surrogates,' multi-context use, and feedback-stabilized control, but it leaves open how to distinguish true aboutness from sophisticated but still non-semantic control architectures.

How can semantic content be individuated and made determinate if intentionality is diffusely realized across a whole architecture rather than locally grounded? The conjecture explains why meaning is not in isolated components, but it remains unclear how a system's representations avoid indeterminacy, misassignment, or observer-relative interpretation when many downstream uses and feedback loops jointly fix content.

Can the 'structurally underprovided coordination good' model generate novel empirical or computational predictions that outperform rival naturalistic theories of intentionality? For the conjecture to mature beyond metaphor, it needs testable consequences about when shared representations emerge, how they degrade, and what interventions should increase or decrease intentionality in biological or artificial systems.
